experiments.reasoning: The passage describes a deliberate observation of a luminous phenomenon in a controlled setting (a darkened room) involving a specific piece of meat (a neck of veal). The author describes a procedure (placing the meat in a dark corner), systematic observations (seeing the meat shine), and a hypothesis-driven curiosity about the phenomenon, fitting the criteria of an experiment.
experiments.description: The experiment involved observing the luminous properties of a neck of veal that had been hung in a dark room. The author describes seeing the meat emit a luminous glow resembling that of rotten wood or stinking fish, likely due to biochemical processes such as bacterial action or chemical decomposition.
experiments.quote: ‘I plainly saw, both with wonder and delight, that the joint of meat did in divers places shine like rotten Wood or stinking Fish’
experiments.outcome: The phenomenon of luminous meat was observed, though the exact cause or mechanism was not explicitly detailed in the passage.
